title: Bug: jit issue breakdown --description splits on commas, corrupts multi-paragraph descriptions
description:
## Summary

`jit issue breakdown --child-type task --subtask "T1" --subtask "T2" --description "desc one, with commas" --description "desc two"` silently splits each `--description` value on commas before zipping them 1:1 against `--subtask` entries. Multi-paragraph descriptions get shredded into fragments and paired with the wrong subtasks.

## Reproduction

```bash
jit issue breakdown <parent-id> --child-type task \
  --subtask "Task A" \
  --subtask "Task B" \
  --description "This is A. It mentions X, Y, and Z." \
  --description "This is B. Short."
```

Expected: Task A gets description "This is A. It mentions X, Y, and Z.", Task B gets "This is B. Short."

Actual: the first description is split on its commas into {"This is A. It mentions X", " Y", " and Z."}, then the flat fragment list zips to subtasks — Task A gets only "This is A. It mentions X", Task B gets " Y", and the trailing fragments silently drop on the floor.

## How it was discovered

Hit on 2026-04-24 while breaking down story abfd6016 into four tasks (multi-format document rendering). All four task descriptions were corrupted on creation and had to be re-set via `jit issue update --description` — which takes a single string correctly and has no such bug.

## Root cause (hypothesis)

The clap `#[arg]` attribute on the `SUBTASK_DESCRIPTIONS` field in `jit issue breakdown` almost certainly has (or inherits) `value_delimiter = ','`. Because the flag is `Vec<String>` with multi-occurrence semantics, the delimiter splits within each occurrence before the count is compared to `--subtask`. Remove the delimiter for this field (descriptions are prose — commas belong inside the value).

Relevant files to check:
- `crates/jit/src/cli.rs` — clap struct for the breakdown subcommand
- `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs` — breakdown handler wiring

## Acceptance criteria

- `jit issue breakdown ... --description "Prose, with, commas."` preserves the description verbatim in the created subtask.
- Multiple `--description` flags with comma-containing values each map 1:1 to the corresponding `--subtask`.
- Regression test in integration tests covers: two subtasks, two descriptions each containing at least two commas.
- `jit issue update --description` behavior unchanged (already correct).

## Related

- Story abfd6016 (multi-format doc rendering) — triggered discovery; descriptions there have already been repaired.
